exampleSentence: El artista contó con el apoyo de un generoso mecenas.

usage.note: A patron who funds the arts (from Maecenas); same form sg/pl. patrocinador is the modern, commercial term.

exampleSentence: La novela destaca por su cuidada narrativa.

usage.note: Narrative — both the craft of storytelling and, in modern usage, a political 'narrative' or spin. relato is a single story.

exampleSentence: La película apuesta por una estética minimalista.

usage.note: Aesthetics or a visual style ('una estética minimalista'); also the adjective estético/a, and the beauty-salon sense (centro de estética).

exampleSentence: Conviene acotar bien el alcance del estudio.

usage.note: Narrow down or delimit scope (a study, topic, quote); also to annotate. delimitar is close, acotar adds a 'fence in' sense.
examples: El comité acotó el alcance de la investigación.

exampleSentence: Cotejaron las dos versiones para hallar diferencias.

usage.note: Cross-check two versions or documents side by side; formal. comparar is general, cotejar implies careful collation.
examples: El editor coteja cada cita con el original.

definition: to prolong, to delay; to dilate
exampleSentence: No conviene dilatar más la decisión.

usage.note: Drag out or postpone (a decision) — reflexive dilatarse = to drag on; also physically to dilate (pupils). retrasar is plainer.
examples: El trámite se dilató varios meses sin explicación.

exampleSentence: Dio una negativa tajante, sin lugar a réplica.

usage.note: Blunt and categorical, allowing no reply (a 'no', a tone); near rotundo, but tajante adds a cutting edge.

exampleSentence: Ofreció una explicación diáfana, sin la menor ambigüedad.

usage.note: Crystal-clear — of light/air/water, and figuratively of prose or explanations; more literary than claro, with a sense of luminous transparency.

exampleSentence: El valle conserva su belleza prístina.

usage.note: Pristine and untouched, esp. of nature or an original state; literary. 'belleza/estado prístino'. puro is the plain word.

exampleSentence: Sintió una alegría inefable al volver a casa.

usage.note: So overwhelming it can't be put into words (joy, beauty); elevated/literary. indescriptible is the everyday equivalent.

exampleSentence: La fama suele ser tan efímera como intensa.

usage.note: Lasting only a moment (fame, beauty, pleasure); fugaz is close, efímero stresses brief existence. Opposite of perenne.

exampleSentence: Su obra goza de una vigencia perenne.

usage.note: Everlasting; also botanically perennial (de hoja perenne). perpetuo/imperecedero are close. Opposite of efímero.

exampleSentence: Redactó un informe sucinto pero completo.

usage.note: Briefly complete — saying all that's needed in few words; conciso is close, sucinto adds economy. Opposite of prolijo.

exampleSentence: El discurso resultó prolijo y algo tedioso.

usage.note: Tiresomely long-winded or over-detailed; negative, the opposite of sucinto. (In parts of Latin America also = meticulously neat.)

exampleSentence: Su respuesta fue escueta y tajante.

usage.note: Terse and bare-bones, stripped of anything extra; parco is close. Of statements, answers, style.

exampleSentence: Aportó pruebas contundentes ante el tribunal.

usage.note: Forceful and leaving no doubt — proof, an argument, a victory, a blow; rotundo is close, contundente adds weight and impact.

exampleSentence: Presentó un documento fehaciente de la transacción.

usage.note: Providing irrefutable proof; formal/legal. 'de forma fehaciente' = verifiably. fidedigno is close.

exampleSentence: Terminó con el consabido agradecimiento al público.

usage.note: The well-known, expected one already understood by all — often with mild irony about its predictability. habitual is neutral.

exampleSentence: Tenía la inveterada costumbre de madrugar.

usage.note: Long-standing and deeply ingrained, esp. habits; arraigado is close, inveterado stresses age. Often faintly negative.

exampleSentence: El plazo perentorio no admitía demoras.

usage.note: Pressingly urgent, brooking no delay; in law, a final deadline. apremiante is close, perentorio more categorical.

exampleSentence: Disponían de un presupuesto exiguo para la obra.

usage.note: Too small to suffice — budget, salary, number; escaso is close, exiguo more formal/literary.

exampleSentence: Reunir el archivo exigió un esfuerzo ingente.

usage.note: Vast in scale or effort; literary. enorme is plainer. Often 'un esfuerzo/una cantidad ingente'.

exampleSentence: La diferencia entre ambos resultados es ínfima.

usage.note: The very lowest or tiniest; superlative in feel. mínimo is close, ínfimo more emphatic. Opposite of máximo.

exampleSentence: Organizaron un magno acto conmemorativo.

usage.note: Grand or great, in solemn set phrases (magno acto, magna obra, Alejandro Magno); fixed, elevated register.

exampleSentence: Lo miró de soslayo, sin atreverse a hablar.

usage.note: Almost always 'de soslayo' = sidelong / out of the corner of one's eye, or evasively. Same idea as 'de reojo'.

exampleSentence: No había en su rostro el menor atisbo de duda.

usage.note: A faint first trace of something (doubt, hope, a smile); 'ni el menor atisbo de' = not the slightest hint of. indicio is close.

exampleSentence: Solo quedan vestigios de la antigua muralla.

usage.note: A surviving trace of something now gone (ruins, customs); often plural. resto is plainer, vestigio more evocative.

exampleSentence: La paz perpetua le parecía una quimera.

usage.note: An impossible dream chased in vain; literary. utopía is close but more political/ideal. 'perseguir quimeras'.

exampleSentence: Aquella sociedad ideal era una pura utopía.

usage.note: An ideal but unattainable society or goal; quimera is close, utopía carries the political/philosophical sense. Adjective: utópico.

exampleSentence: Actuó por su libre albedrío, sin presiones.

usage.note: Free will / one's own discretion; almost always 'libre albedrío' or 'a su albedrío' (as one pleases). voluntad is plainer.

exampleSentence: El humor forma parte de la idiosincrasia del país.

usage.note: The distinctive character of a people or person; formal. carácter/identidad are close. Adjective: idiosincrásico.

exampleSentence: Es menester revisar el contrato antes de firmarlo.

usage.note: Two uses: 'es menester + inf.' = it is necessary to (formal 'hay que'); and plural 'menesteres' = tasks/duties. necesidad is plainer.

exampleSentence: La distancia no fue óbice para mantener la amistad.

usage.note: An obstacle — almost always 'no ser óbice para' = to be no bar to. impedimento is the plain word.

exampleSentence: Defendió sus ideas sin menoscabo de las ajenas.

usage.note: Damage to something valued; 'en/sin menoscabo de' = to/without the detriment of. The noun of menoscabar; perjuicio is plainer.

exampleSentence: Aceptó el plan con una única salvedad.

usage.note: A caveat attached to an agreement; 'con la salvedad de que...' = with the proviso that. excepción is close.

definition: look, aspect (that a situation takes on)
exampleSentence: El conflicto tomó un cariz preocupante.

usage.note: The look or turn a situation is taking; 'tomar mal/buen cariz'. aspecto is plainer, cariz implies a developing trend.

exampleSentence: En aquella difícil tesitura, optó por callar.

usage.note: A tricky situation one is placed in (also a voice's range in music); 'en esta tesitura'. coyuntura is close.

exampleSentence: Dejó su futuro al albur de una sola decisión.

usage.note: Pure chance/risk; 'al albur de' = at the mercy of. azar is plainer, albur stresses the gamble. (In Mexico also a pun.)

exampleSentence: Descubrieron el ardid antes de que surtiera efecto.

usage.note: A clever ruse to deceive or gain advantage; artimaña/treta are close. Neutral-to-negative.

exampleSentence: Recurrió a un subterfugio para eludir la pregunta.

usage.note: An evasive dodge to avoid something; evasiva/pretexto are close, subterfugio more formal. 'recurrir a subterfugios'.

exampleSentence: Sus argucias legales retrasaron el juicio.

usage.note: A specious, cunning argument — esp. legal hair-splitting; sofisma is close. Pejorative.

exampleSentence: Actuaron en connivencia con las autoridades locales.

usage.note: Secret collusion in wrongdoing; 'en connivencia con' = in cahoots with. complicidad is close.

exampleSentence: La fehaciencia del documento quedó acreditada.

usage.note: The quality of being irrefutable proof; rare, formal/legal noun of fehaciente. autenticidad is close.

exampleSentence: El árbitro se mostró ecuánime en todo momento.

usage.note: Even-handed and composed under pressure; imparcial stresses fairness, ecuánime adds calm balance. Noun: ecuanimidad.

exampleSentence: Respondió con un tono displicente y altivo.

usage.note: Coolly offhand, showing bored disdain; desdeñoso is close. Of tone, gesture, manner.

exampleSentence: Su gesto altanero molestó a los presentes.

usage.note: Haughtily superior; altivo/soberbio are close. Of look, gesture, bearing.

exampleSentence: No fue pusilánime: defendió su postura con firmeza.

usage.note: Lacking the courage to act or decide; cobarde is the blunt word, pusilánime is formal and weaker-willed.

exampleSentence: Se mostró magnánimo con sus adversarios.

usage.note: Nobly generous, esp. to rivals or the defeated; generoso is plainer, magnánimo adds greatness of spirit. Noun: magnanimidad.

exampleSentence: Una observadora sagaz no pasaría por alto ese detalle.

usage.note: Shrewd at reading people and situations; perspicaz/astuto are close, sagaz adds cunning foresight.

exampleSentence: Desde la noticia, se volvió huraño y taciturno.

usage.note: Habitually silent and withdrawn, often gloomy; callado describes the moment, taciturno the temperament.

exampleSentence: El invitado, muy locuaz, animó la tertulia.

usage.note: Very talkative, with ready fluency; parlanchín is colloquial, locuaz more neutral. Opposite of taciturno.

exampleSentence: Su carácter veleidoso le impedía mantener un plan.

usage.note: Fickle, forever changing whims and loyalties; voluble/inconstante are close. Of character, affections.

exampleSentence: Hizo un esfuerzo denodado por terminar a tiempo.

usage.note: Valiantly strenuous — of effort, defence, struggle; literary. esforzado is close, denodado adds fearlessness.

exampleSentence: Recordaron la abnegada labor del personal sanitario.

usage.note: Selflessly devoted, putting others first; altruista is close, abnegado stresses sacrifice. Often of carers, parents.

exampleSentence: Permaneció impávido ante la amenaza.

usage.note: Showing no fear or reaction in the face of danger; imperturbable is close. 'permanecer impávido'.

exampleSentence: Conservó hasta el final su espíritu indómito.

usage.note: That cannot be tamed or subdued — spirit, character, nature; rebelde is plainer, indómito more literary and grand.

exampleSentence: Solo con tenacidad alcanzó semejante meta.

usage.note: Dogged persistence toward a goal; perseverancia/constancia are close. 'con tenacidad'.

exampleSentence: Analizó el caso con notable perspicacia.

usage.note: Keen insight into what isn't obvious; agudeza/lucidez are close. The noun of perspicaz.

exampleSentence: El traductor supo captar cada sutileza del texto.

usage.note: A fine distinction or delicate touch; matiz is close. 'captar las sutilezas'.

exampleSentence: Defendió su tesis con admirable elocuencia.

usage.note: Persuasive eloquence in speech; oratoria is the formal art, labia the colloquial gift of the gab.

exampleSentence: Su verbosidad acabó por cansar al auditorio.

usage.note: An excess of words for little content; pejorative. palabrería is colloquial, prolijidad close. Opposite of concisión.

exampleSentence: Hablaba con una parquedad casi solemne.

usage.note: Sparingness — of words or means; 'parquedad de palabras'. sobriedad is close. From parco.

exampleSentence: Actuó siempre con mesura y prudencia.

usage.note: Calm moderation in conduct or speech; comedimiento is close. 'con mesura'. Opposite of exceso.

exampleSentence: La templanza es una de sus mayores virtudes.

usage.note: Self-control over impulses and appetites — a classical virtue; moderación is close, templanza more moral/elevated.

exampleSentence: Defendió su idea con inusitada vehemencia.

usage.note: Passionate intensity in expression; pasión/ardor are close. 'con vehemencia' = forcefully, heatedly.

exampleSentence: Estudió con ahínco hasta dominar la materia.

usage.note: Earnest effort and drive; almost always 'con ahínco' = eagerly, hard. empeño/tesón are close.

exampleSentence: Afrontó la crisis con denuedo y serenidad.

usage.note: Fearless boldness in facing difficulty; literary. valentía/arrojo are close. 'con denuedo'.

exampleSentence: Vivía con el anhelo de regresar a su tierra.

usage.note: A deep, often unfulfilled yearning; deseo is plainer, anhelo more intense and heartfelt. Verb: anhelar.

exampleSentence: La noticia le produjo una profunda congoja.

usage.note: Deep anguish/heartache, with a choking quality; pesar/aflicción are close. Literary-emotional.

exampleSentence: Sentía una extraña desazón sin saber por qué.

usage.note: A vague unease you can't quite name; inquietud is close, desazón adds a queasy discomfort.

exampleSentence: El hastío de la rutina lo empujó a cambiar de vida.

usage.note: Weary boredom verging on disgust; tedio is close, hastío adds satiety/revulsion. 'el hastío de la rutina'.

exampleSentence: La victoria fue motivo de regocijo general.

usage.note: Lively, expressed joy or glee; júbilo is close. 'motivo de regocijo'. Often collective.

exampleSentence: Recibieron la noticia con enorme júbilo.

usage.note: Exultant joy, often public; alegría is plainer, júbilo more intense and elevated. 'estallar en júbilo'.

exampleSentence: Buscaba el sosiego del campo los fines de semana.

usage.note: Peaceful calm of mind or place; calma/serenidad are close. 'buscar sosiego'. From sosegar.

exampleSentence: Pasó la noche en una constante zozobra.

usage.note: Restless anxiety/turmoil (literally a boat capsizing); angustia is close. 'vivir en la zozobra'.

exampleSentence: En un arrebato de ira, rompió el documento.

usage.note: A sudden fit of strong emotion driving rash action; 'en un arrebato de ira/pasión'. impulso is plainer.

exampleSentence: Acometió el proyecto con renovado ímpetu.

usage.note: Driving force and momentum; energía/brío are close, ímpetu adds forward thrust. 'con ímpetu'.

exampleSentence: Defendió la causa con verdadero fervor.

usage.note: Burning zeal or devotion (a cause, a faith); pasión/entusiasmo are close. 'con fervor'.

exampleSentence: Con tesón y paciencia, levantó el negocio.

usage.note: Steady grit that keeps going; constancia/tenacidad are close. 'con tesón'.

exampleSentence: Decidieron acometer una reforma de gran calado.

usage.note: Take on a task boldly and with energy; emprender is neutral, acometer adds vigour and onset. Also 'acometer contra' = to charge at.
examples: El equipo acometió las obras con energía renovada.

exampleSentence: Ese asunto atañe a todos los socios por igual.

usage.note: To concern / be the business of — 'eso me atañe'; defective, used only in the 3rd person. concernir/incumbir are close.
examples: Esas decisiones atañen únicamente a la dirección.

exampleSentence: El tribunal habrá de dirimir la controversia.

usage.note: To settle a dispute or conflict authoritatively; zanjar is close but blunter, dirimir more formal/legal. 'dirimir diferencias'.
examples: Un segundo árbitro dirimió el empate.

definition: to settle, to put an end to
exampleSentence: El director quiso zanjar la discusión de inmediato.

usage.note: To put a definitive end to a discussion or matter; resolver is neutral, zanjar implies cutting it off decisively.
examples: Con una sola frase zanjó toda polémica.

definition: to stick to, to confine oneself to
exampleSentence: Le pidieron ceñirse estrictamente a los hechos.

usage.note: To stick strictly to limits or a topic — 'ceñirse a los hechos / al guion'; limitarse/atenerse are close.
examples: Siempre se ceñía al guion establecido.

exampleSentence: El orador soslayó con habilidad los temas espinosos.

usage.note: To sidestep or skirt a difficulty/question deftly; eludir is close, soslayar adds going around it. Cf. 'de soslayo'.
examples: El ministro soslaya siempre las preguntas espinosas.

exampleSentence: Una vieja rivalidad subyace en el conflicto.

usage.note: To lie underneath as a hidden cause or idea; 'subyacer en/a algo'. Almost always 3rd person.
examples: Bajo su aparente calma subyacía una gran tensión.

exampleSentence: El estudio pretende dilucidar el origen del fenómeno.

usage.note: To clear up something obscure through reasoning; esclarecer/aclarar are close, dilucidar more intellectual and formal.
examples: Los expertos aún no han dilucidado la causa.

definition: to be dimly perceived, to loom
exampleSentence: Comienza a vislumbrarse una salida a la crisis.

usage.note: To be dimly perceived on the horizon (a solution, an end); the pronominal of vislumbrar, used impersonally. entreverse is close.
examples: Por fin se vislumbra el final del túnel.

definition: to get it right, to hit the mark
exampleSentence: Atinó con la respuesta a la primera.

usage.note: To hit the mark / get it right, by good judgement or luck; 'atinar con la respuesta', 'atinar a + inf.'. acertar is the everyday word.
examples: No atinó a responder en aquel momento.

definition: to ponder; to flow, to pass (time)
exampleSentence: La conversación discurrió por cauces amistosos.

usage.note: Two senses: to ponder/reason; and (of time, talks, a river) to flow/pass. transcurrir covers time, reflexionar the thinking.
examples: La velada discurrió sin incidentes.

exampleSentence: El autor ahonda en las raíces del problema.

usage.note: To delve deeper into a topic (or a wound); 'ahondar en' = to go further into. profundizar is the close everyday word.
examples: El segundo capítulo ahondó en sus motivaciones.

exampleSentence: No conviene extrapolar conclusiones de un solo caso.

usage.note: To project findings beyond their data or context; technical. 'extrapolar a partir de'. generalizar is plainer.
examples: Extrapolaron los resultados a toda la población.

definition: to spoil, to take the shine off
exampleSentence: La lluvia deslució por completo la ceremonia.

usage.note: To dull the shine or spoil the effect of an event; estropear is plainer, empañar close. 'deslucir una ceremonia'.
examples: Unos incidentes deslucieron la celebración.

exampleSentence: Sería un error menospreciar al rival.

usage.note: To look down on or undervalue; despreciar is stronger (open scorn), subestimar = to underestimate.
examples: Tiende a menospreciar a quienes no piensan como él.

exampleSentence: El monumento busca enaltecer la memoria de los caídos.

usage.note: To exalt or honour, raising someone's standing; ensalzar is close, glorificar stronger. Opposite of denigrar.
examples: Sus actos enaltecieron a toda la institución.

exampleSentence: No es propio de un profesional denostar al contrario.

usage.note: To revile with harsh words; injuriar/vilipendiar are close. Formal/literary.
examples: Lo denostaron sin pruebas en la prensa.

definition: to clarify, to shed light on
exampleSentence: La investigación logró esclarecer los hechos.

usage.note: To shed light on / clear up facts, esp. in an investigation; aclarar is plainer, dilucidar more intellectual.
examples: La autopsia esclareció las causas de la muerte.

exampleSentence: Surgió una notable discrepancia entre los informes.

usage.note: A divergence or disagreement between views or figures; divergencia/desacuerdo are close. 'discrepancias entre'.

exampleSentence: Actuó en consonancia con sus principios.

usage.note: Agreement or harmony between things; almost always 'en consonancia con' = in keeping with. concordancia is close.

exampleSentence: El tratado pretende salvaguardar los derechos humanos.

usage.note: To protect or safeguard rights, interests, secrets; proteger is plainer, salvaguardar more formal/legal. Noun: salvaguarda.
examples: El acuerdo salvaguarda los intereses de ambas partes.

definition: to subordinate, to make conditional on
exampleSentence: Supeditó su decisión al resultado de la votación.

usage.note: To make one thing conditional on / subordinate to another; 'supeditar A a B'. subordinar/condicionar are close.
examples: Supeditaron la ayuda a ciertas reformas.

exampleSentence: El informe preconiza un cambio de modelo energético.

usage.note: To publicly advocate or recommend a course of action; 'abogar por' is close, preconizar more formal. 'preconizar un cambio'.
examples: Desde hace años preconizan un modelo más sostenible.

exampleSentence: Pocos se atrevieron a vaticinar semejante desenlace.

usage.note: To foretell or predict, often boldly; pronosticar is neutral/technical, augurar close. Noun: vaticinio.
examples: Los analistas vaticinaron una caída de las ventas.

exampleSentence: Desde la cima se atisbaba el mar a lo lejos.

usage.note: To glimpse or barely make out (also to spy/peek); vislumbrar is close. Both literal (atisbar el mar) and figurative.
examples: Apenas se atisbaban las luces del pueblo.

exampleSentence: Pasó la tarde deambulando por las callejuelas.

usage.note: To wander aimlessly; vagar/errar are close. 'deambular por' = to roam around.
examples: Deambuló un buen rato hasta dar con la plaza.

exampleSentence: Visitaron un rincón recóndito de la sierra.

usage.note: Hidden away and remote — a place, or a deep feeling ('lo más recóndito del alma'); escondido/apartado are plainer.

exampleSentence: Acamparon en un paraje solitario junto al río.

usage.note: A spot or place, usually natural or scenic; lugar/sitio are plainer, paraje evokes wild or solitary settings.

exampleSentence: Fue un día aciago que nadie quiso recordar.

usage.note: Fateful and unlucky — a day, date, omen; funesto is close. Literary. Opposite of venturoso.

exampleSentence: El encuentro fue del todo fortuito.

usage.note: Happening purely by chance; casual/accidental are close. 'caso fortuito' is a legal term (act of God).

exampleSentence: Asumir el cambio era ya algo ineludible.

usage.note: That cannot be avoided or escaped — a duty, fact, deadline; inevitable is close, ineludible stresses obligation.

exampleSentence: El conflicto era difícilmente soslayable.

usage.note: Able to be sidestepped — usually negated ('difícilmente soslayable'); rare, from soslayar. evitable is the plain word.

exampleSentence: Ofrecieron una cantidad irrisoria por la casa.

usage.note: So small or poor as to be laughable, esp. a sum or offer; ridículo is close. 'una cantidad irrisoria'.

exampleSentence: La empresa atraviesa una etapa boyante.

usage.note: Thriving and prosperous — economy, business; próspero/floreciente are close. (Originally of a buoyant ship.)

exampleSentence: Su influencia, antes enorme, es ya menguante.

usage.note: Waning or decreasing — influence, the moon (luna menguante); decreciente is close. Opposite of creciente. From menguar.

exampleSentence: Lidera un sector pujante y lleno de talento.

usage.note: Vigorously growing — a sector, economy, movement; floreciente/vigoroso are close, pujante adds surging momentum.

exampleSentence: Convirtió su afición en un negocio lucrativo.

usage.note: Bringing good profit; rentable is close, lucrativo can hint at money-driven. Cf. 'sin ánimo de lucro' (non-profit).

definition: to defray, to cover the cost of
exampleSentence: Una beca sufragó por completo sus estudios.

usage.note: To pay for / cover the cost of (studies, expenses); costear/financiar are close. (Also 'sufragar' = to vote, in some contexts.)
examples: El ayuntamiento sufragará las obras del nuevo parque.

exampleSentence: El museo nació de la munificencia de un coleccionista.

usage.note: Lavish, lordly generosity, esp. of a patron; generosidad is plainer, munificencia formal/literary. Cf. mecenas.

exampleSentence: Vivía con una austeridad casi monacal.

usage.note: Severe plainness or thrift, by choice or policy; sobriedad/frugalidad are close. 'medidas de austeridad'.

exampleSentence: Le prodigaron toda clase de atenciones.

usage.note: To lavish (attention, praise) freely; reflexive 'prodigarse en' = to be generous with / appear often. derrochar/colmar are close.
examples: Rara vez prodiga elogios sin un buen motivo.

exampleSentence: Restauró el cuadro con sumo esmero.

usage.note: Painstaking care put into doing something well; cuidado/dedicación are close. 'con (sumo) esmero'.

exampleSentence: Maniobró con la pericia de un veterano.

usage.note: Practised skill or expertise at a craft; destreza/maestría are close. 'con pericia'. Adjective: perito (expert).

exampleSentence: Tocó la pieza con absoluta maestría.

usage.note: Consummate mastery of an art or skill; pericia is close, maestría implies the highest command. (In Latin America also a master's degree.)

exampleSentence: La escasez de agua es un problema acuciante.

usage.note: Pressing and demanding immediate attention — a need, problem; apremiante/urgente are close. From acuciar.

exampleSentence: Denunció el soslayo cultural de las lenguas minoritarias.

usage.note: Set phrase = the sidelining/neglect of something cultural; built on 'soslayo' (see soslayo). Used in critique and journalism.

exampleSentence: Realizó una labor encomiable durante la crisis.

usage.note: Deserving of praise — effort, attitude, work; loable is synonymous, both formal. 'una labor encomiable'.

exampleSentence: Es loable su empeño por mejorar las cosas.

usage.note: Praiseworthy — intentions, efforts; encomiable/meritorio are close. 'un empeño loable'.

exampleSentence: No era un asunto baladí, sino crucial.

usage.note: Trivial, of no importance — often negated for emphasis ('no es asunto baladí'). trivial/insignificante are close.

exampleSentence: Un giro inopinado cambió el rumbo de los hechos.

usage.note: Unexpected and unforeseen; inesperado/imprevisto are close, inopinado more literary. 'un giro inopinado'.

exampleSentence: Bajo la calma latía un descontento soterrado.

usage.note: Hidden beneath the surface — a conflict, discontent; oculto/encubierto are close. From soterrar. 'un malestar soterrado'.

exampleSentence: Profesaba un acendrado amor por su tierra.

usage.note: Pure and deeply held — of feelings or virtues ('acendrado amor/patriotismo'); puro/arraigado are close, acendrado is literary and refined.
